metadata.source: A Gazetteer of the World (c.1856), Vol. 5

properties.name: Aar
notes: Lutschine joins it in the Lake of Brienz

properties.name: Agra
notes: Mahratta dominion reached from here to Cape Comorin

properties.name: Baroach
notes: capital of Guicowar's sovereignty

properties.name: Bassein
notes: Peishwa took refuge here

properties.name: Bejapore
properties.feature_term: Mahommedan king of Bejapore or Vislapore (kingdom)
notes: last Mahommedan kingdom

properties.name: Benares
notes: Peishwa was allowed to retire here

properties.name: Bishop's Lydiard
notes: Includes the tythings of Bishop's-Lydiard, Coombe-Ash, East Bagborough, East Coomb-Hill, Lydiard-Punchardon, and Quantock

properties.name: Bombay
notes: Ragobah took refuge here
Bombay government made a treaty with the Peishwa

properties.name: Cape Comorin
notes: Mahratta dominion reached from Prov. of Agra to here

properties.name: City of Luxemburg
properties.feature_term: city of L. shall be considered in a military point of view as a fortress of the confederation
properties.admin: Grand Duchy of Luxemburg › German confederation
notes: Considered in a military point of view as a fortress of the confederation
Included in the eastern portion of the Grand Duchy assigned to Holland

properties.name: Delhi
notes: Mahratta conquests extended to here

properties.name: El Maabdeh
notes: Has 5 Coptic churches
Near extensive caverns which seem to have been employed as places of sepulture for the mummies of crocodiles

properties.name: Grand Duchy of Luxemburg
notes: Formed an integral part of ancient Belgium
Had no special relations with Germany previous to 1814
Served as compensation for the principalities of Nassau-Dillenburg, Siegen, Hadamar, and Dietz
Sovereignty formed a subject of dispute between Belgium and Holland

properties.name: Hoi
notes: founder of Holkar family filled a local office here

properties.name: Indore
notes: capital of Holkar's sovereignty

properties.name: Karnak
notes: Adjacent to Luxor
Stands on the site of the ancient Thebes
Possesses magnificent ruins

properties.name: Khandeish
notes: original territory of the Mahrattas

properties.name: Lake Of Brienz
notes: Lutschine and Aar join here

properties.name: Libyan Mountain
notes: Limits the field of ruins of ancient Thebes on the NW
Lofty and picturesque pile
Royal and celebrated tombs are excavated here

properties.name: Luristan
notes: richest and most fruitful part of Irak
abundantly watered by numerous streams
agriculture neglected except in vicinity of certain places
natives are rude, wandering, and pastoral

properties.name: Luristan-Buzurg
notes: separated from Luristan-Kuchuk by the Dizful River

properties.name: Luristan-Kuchuk
notes: separated from Luristan-Buzurg by the Dizful River
subdivided into Pishkuh and Pusht-i-kuh

properties.name: Lusignan
notes: Noted for its macaroons
Manufactories of coarse woollen fabrics
Several tanneries
Dye-work

properties.name: Lusignan-Le-Petit
notes: on the right bank of the Garonne

properties.name: Lusigny
notes: The canton comprises 14 communes
Bees are extensively reared in the environs
Trade consists chiefly in cattle

properties.name: Lusk
notes: Dublin and Drogheda railway passes across its interior

properties.name: Lusk
notes: Church is very old
Church in pointed style of architecture

properties.name: Lusmagh
notes: containing the hamlet of Stream, and the villages of Newtown and Lower Newtown

properties.name: Lussac
notes: Comprises 15 communes

properties.name: Lussac
notes: Environs afford good wine

properties.name: Lussac-Les-Eglises
notes: on the Lasse river

properties.name: Lussac-les-Chateaux
notes: comprises 13 communes

properties.name: Lussac-les-Chateaux
notes: on the right bank of the Vienne
has tanneries and rope-works
environs are several quarries of excellent freestone
Hemp forms the chief article of local agriculture

properties.name: Lussan
properties.feature_term: canton and commune of France, and village
notes: canton comprises 12 communes
on the right bank of the Aquillon

properties.name: Lussat
properties.admin: department of the Creuse › canton of Chambon

properties.name: Lussat
properties.admin: department of the Puy-de-Dome › canton of Pont-du-Chateau

properties.name: Lussin Piccolo
notes: safe and commodious harbour
active trade in figs and oranges
extensive fisheries

properties.name: Lussow
properties.admin: grand-duchy of Mecklenburg-Schwerin › duchy of Mecklenburg-Gustrow › Gustrow bailiwick

properties.name: Lustenau
notes: on the right bank of the Rhine

properties.name: Lustra
notes: Pigs reared in large numbers

properties.name: Lutgenburg
notes: one of the most ancient towns in the duchy
has several distilleries
possesses a small port
trade consists chiefly in the agricultural productions of the locality

properties.name: Lutisburg
properties.admin: Canton of St. Gall › District of Lower Toggenburg
notes: on the Thur
manufactories of cotton and woollen fabrics
castle in the environs

properties.name: Lutomersk
notes: in a mountainous locality
on the Left bank of the Ner River
has a Calvinist convent
possesses manufactories of varieties of silk and woollen fabrics

properties.name: Luton
notes: Comprising the hamlets of East Hyde, West Hyde, Leagrave or Lightgrave, Limbury-cum-Biscott, and Stopsley

properties.name: Luton
notes: Pleasantly situated on the banks of the Lea
Consists principally of three streets, diverging obliquely from the market-house
Manufacture of straw-plait is carried on
Malting establishments

properties.name: Lutry
notes: Protestant population
small but beautifully situated town
has a fine walk planted with lime trees, named the Grand Pont
surrounding district affords excellent wine

properties.name: Lutschine
notes: formed by the confluence of the Weisse-Lutschine and the Schwarze-Lutschine
joins the Aar in the Lake of Brienz
receives the Staubbach and Schmadribach

properties.name: Lutsellus
properties.admin: prov. of Limburg › dep. of Diepenbeck

properties.name: Luttenberg
notes: on the right bank of the Stainzbach
environs noted for their wine

properties.name: Lutter-am-Barenberge
notes: several spinning-mills
oil and sawmills
active trade
quarries of free-stone

properties.name: Lutterbach
notes: In an island formed by the Dollerin, an affluent of the Ill
Has a manufactory of printed calico

properties.name: Lutterberg
properties.admin: Hanover › Principality of Göttingen › Government of Hildesheim › Bailiwick

properties.name: Lutterworth
notes: on the Midland counties railway
chiefly employed in the manufacture of hosiery and ribbons

properties.name: Lutzelburg
properties.admin: Department of Meurthe › Canton of Phalsbourg
notes: on the right bank of the Sarre
has an oil-mill
has several mineral springs
ruins of a castle on an adjacent height

properties.name: Lutzen
notes: Has a suburb, 2 churches, and an hospital
Site of two memorable battles (1632, 1813)

properties.name: Luxan
properties.admin: Government of Buenos Ayres › Province of Buenos Ayres
notes: has its source in a small lake
runs E past a town of the same name
falls into the Plata, on the r. bank

properties.name: Luxemburg
notes: One of the strongest cities in Europe
Fortress belonging to the German confederation
Governor's castle is the only building worthy of notice
Town and environs interesting to the antiquary from remains of antiquity

properties.name: Luxemburg
notes: lies in the basins of the Meuse and Moselle
intersected by several branches of the Ardennes
Principal rivers are the Ourte, the Roche, the Semoi, the Lesse, the Moselle, the Sure, and the Alzette
soil is in great part stony, and consists of only a thin bed of vegetable mould

properties.name: Luxeuil
notes: Near the right bank of the Brenchin, here crossed by a fine bridge.
Altitude of 1,002 ft. above sea-level.
Noted for its mineral baths.
Has a college and a large hospital.

properties.name: Luxey
notes: Has a blast-furnace
Has a forge
Has a foundery
Has a glass-house

properties.name: Luxor
notes: On the right bank of the Nile
Pigeons in large numbers are reared in the locality
Stands on the site of the ancient Thebes
Contains numerous remains of the ancient city

properties.name: Luy-Chu-Fu
notes: comprises 3 districts

properties.name: Luya
notes: on the right bank of the Utcubamba River

properties.name: Luya And Chillaos
notes: watered by the Utcubamba and other smaller streams
produces grain, fruit, cotton, tobacco, sugar, and cacao
climate is mild, with perpetual spring in some parts
horses, mules, and cattle reared on pastures

properties.name: Luynes
notes: Has manufactories of parchment, ribbon, velvet, and flowers
Has a brick-work
Wax forms its chief article of trade

properties.name: Lydford (West)
notes: on the river Brue

properties.name: Lydoch (Loch)
notes: in the moor of Rannoch
7 m. in length
1 m. in breadth

properties.name: Lygumkloster
notes: Projected Danish peninsular railway passes near this town

properties.name: Lyme-Regis
notes: small and irregularly built place
river Lyme passes through the middle of the town and falls into the sea near the fort at the Cobb-gate
houses constructed of blue rag-stone
subject to inundations in spring-tides

properties.name: Lymfiord
notes: Communicates with the Cattegat
Runs west across the peninsula
Stretches southwest
Within a few miles of the German Ocean

properties.name: Lymington
notes: situated on the W bank of the Lymington river
borough income in 1839-40 was £103
borough income in 1849-50 was £115
parliamentary boundaries include Lymington parish and part of Boldre parish

properties.name: Lymington
notes: Hundred comprises the parishes of Uplands, Lymington, Deddington, and Beverley

properties.name: Lympne
notes: Intersected by the Royal Military canal
Formerly a Roman castle, site now occupied by Struttfall-castle
Supposed to have been mentioned by Ptolemy as Limen
Mentioned in Antoninus' Itinerary as Portus Lemanis

properties.name: Lympsham
notes: on the river Axe
in the line of the Bristol and Exeter railway

properties.name: Lympston
notes: on a branch of the Exe

properties.name: Lynchburg
notes: Active trade in tobacco, cotton, and flour
On James river

properties.name: Lynd
notes: Flowing NW through a mountainous country

properties.name: Lyndhurst
notes: village is situated almost in the centre of the New Forest
regarded as the capital of the New Forest
King's-house is the official residence of the lord-warden
King's-stables erected about the time of Charles II

properties.name: Lyndon
notes: on the river Chater

properties.name: Lyne
notes: Next in local importance to the Tweed
Entire length of course is 15.5 m, or, including sinuosities, about 20 m

properties.name: Lyne
notes: Nearly circular with a small square northerly projection
Combined population of Lyne and Megget parishes: 156 in 1831, 175 in 1841

properties.name: Lynn
notes: on Nahant promontory

properties.name: Lynn Canal
notes: named by Vancouver, after the town of Lynn, his native place

properties.name: Lynn-Regis
notes: At the mouth of the Great Ouse, on its east bank
Town stands at the west extremity of a district rising eastward in gentle eminences
The Ouse is nearly 0.25 miles in breadth here
River above this point diverted into a new and more direct channel, called the Eau-brink cut

properties.name: Lynx-Kraal
notes: in the E part of the Koranas country

properties.name: Lyon
notes: Entire length of course is 32 m
Longest tributary is Glenmore-water, 7.5 m in length

properties.name: Lyong
notes: in the Eastern seas

properties.name: Lyonnais
notes: Located in the SE of France
On the west side of the Saone and the Loire rivers
About 30 miles in length and 17 miles in breadth
Fertile in wine, corn, and fruit

properties.name: Lyons
notes: Altitude: 532 ft. above sea-level.
Confluence of the Rhone and Saone rivers.
Surrounded by a rampart, with six gates.
Suburbs: Serin, St. Clair (conjointly La Croix-Rousse), St. Irenée, Vaise, St. Just, St. George's, Faubourg Guillotiere, Quartier-des-Brotteaux.

properties.name: Lyons
notes: on the Erie canal

properties.name: Lyons
notes: at the head of the steam-boat navigation of Grand river

properties.name: Lys
properties.admin: Pas-de-Calais department › Nord department › West Flanders province › East Flanders province
notes: Navigable a distance of 114 miles
Forms an important system of communication by its affluents and canals
Principal affluents are the Deule and Mandelle
Receives Mandelle on the left bank

properties.name: Lysiec
notes: manufactory of marocco leather

properties.name: Lysing
notes: located in the W part of the prefecture

properties.name: Lyskova
notes: Has a seignorial castle and 6 churches
Hosts a large annual fair for horses and cattle

properties.name: Lysobyki
properties.admin: Woiwodship of Siedlec › Obwod of Radzyn
notes: on the right bank of the Wieprz

properties.name: Lyssyia-Gory
notes: on the right bank of the Tchelnovaïa

properties.name: Lyster
properties.admin: diocese of Bergen › bailiwick of North Bergen

properties.name: Lyszkowo
notes: on the left bank of the Niemen

properties.name: Lytham
notes: on the N bank of the estuary of the Ribble
watering-place

properties.name: Lythe
notes: Includes alum works
Mulgrave Castle in vicinity

properties.name: Ma-Bung
notes: on the coast of Sierra Leone
on the left bank of the Rokelli river

properties.name: Maad
notes: in the midst of the Hegyallva chain of hills
has a synagogue and 2 churches (Catholic and Protestant)
noted for its wines and vintage fetes
royal vineyard of Theresienberg in the vicinity

properties.name: Maam
notes: In the glen of the Bealnabrack rivulet
Only practicable inlet to the S, E, W, or central districts of Joyce-country

properties.name: Maam-Ina
notes: Through the group of the Binabola mountains

properties.name: Maan
notes: consists of two parts, built on two hills
possesses fine gardens and vineyards
pomegranates, apricots, and peaches are of the finest quality
inhabitants derive their chief subsistence from pilgrims

properties.name: Maaninga
properties.admin: Grand-duchy of Finland › Government of Kuopio › District of Edre-Savolax

properties.name: Maanjiry
properties.admin: Madras Presidency › Malabar Province › Ernaad District

properties.name: Maanselka
properties.admin: Grand-duchy of Finland › Gov. of Uleaborg
notes: forms a portion of the dividing-line between the White Sea and the Baltic

properties.name: Maarssen
notes: half of whom are Jews
has a synagogue
contains many fine villas
on the l. bank of the Wecht

properties.name: Maasland
notes: Capital was the Hague
Partitioned amongst the French departments of the Deux-Nethes, Bouches-du-Rhin, and Bouches-de-la-Meuse in 1809 and 1810
Now forms the S part of the province of Holland

properties.name: Maasland
notes: Located in the S part of the prov. of Holland

properties.name: Maaslandsluis
notes: on an arm of the Meuse
manufactories of sail-cloth, cordage, leather, and oil
building-docks
herring, cod, and whale-fishing are important local industries

properties.name: Maasoe
notes: has a good port
unhealthy
scantily populated

properties.name: Maat
properties.admin: presidency of Bengal › province of Agra › district of Aligur
notes: on the left bank of the Jumna River

properties.name: Mabba
notes: affluent of the Rio Negro
joins Rio Negro on the right bank

properties.name: Mabbe
notes: On the Left bank of the Rio Negro

properties.name: Mabelly
notes: on the coast

properties.name: Mabruk
notes: houses are substantially built of stone
surrounding country is very fertile
inhabitants are chiefly Tuaricks

properties.name: Mabuyauha
notes: Flows into the Coyari, an affluent of the Içana

properties.name: Macabu
notes: rises in the N flank of the Serra do Frade
runs E to the Lagoa Feia

properties.name: Macacos
notes: Discharges into the Rio-Madeira on its r. bank

properties.name: Macacu
notes: on the left bank of Macacu River
a little above the confluence of Guapyassu River

properties.name: Macacu River
notes: has its source in the Serra Dos Órgãos
tortuous course
generally SW direction
approx. 45 miles long

properties.name: Macahalar
notes: 24 m. in breadth at its entrance
about equal in depth

properties.name: Mahratta Territory
notes: politically extended across what is called the peninsula
original territory said to have been Khandeish
possessed a narrow track of country on the W side of the peninsula, extending from the 15th to the 21st parallel of N lat.
divided into 6 separate independent sovereignties

properties.name: Manosque
notes: Canton comprises 6 communes.
Canton population in 1831, 9,476; in 1841, 9,208.
Altitude of 123 yards above sea-level.
At the foot of a hill on the summit of which are a tower and several ruins, which bear the name of Old Manosque.

properties.name: Marthalen
notes: 8 Catholics in 1850

properties.name: Mazuecos
notes: near the Valdegonate

properties.name: Med-Amud
notes: Adjacent to Luxor
Stands on the site of the ancient Thebes

properties.name: Medinet-Abu
notes: Adjacent to Luxor
Stands on the site of the ancient Thebes

properties.name: Megget
notes: Lies on the S verge of the county
Combined population of Lyne and Megget parishes: 156 in 1831, 175 in 1841

properties.name: Mihla
properties.admin: Grand-duchy of Saxe-Weimar › Principality of Eisenach › Bailiwick of Kreutzburg
notes: manufactories of velvet
agriculture

properties.name: Mormond-Hill
notes: 810 ft. above sea-level
good land-mark to mariners

properties.name: Mulla Ghat
notes: Leading from Kurar to Mahajun

properties.name: Nagpore
notes: capital of Bhonslah's sovereignty
founded a new kingdom here

properties.name: Nile
notes: Vale expands into a broad fertile plain
Ancient Thebes occupied both sides of the river
Mud and drifting sand have filled up the old harbour at Luxor

properties.name: Ojacastro
notes: on the l. bank of the Oja

properties.name: Oojein
notes: capital of Sindia's sovereignty

properties.name: Papinachois
notes: Flows SSW
Course of about 60 miles
Joins the Saguenay on the left bank

properties.name: Paris
notes: Location of Place de Concord

properties.name: Pishkuh
notes: lying east of the Zagros chain

properties.name: Punah
notes: residence and capital of the Peishwa
Peishwa reinstated in May 1803

properties.name: Pusht-i-kuh
notes: lying west of the Zagros chain

properties.name: Saint-Lys
notes: Comprises 11 communes

properties.name: Saint-Lys
notes: Manufactories of linen
Considerable trade in charcoal

properties.name: Saint-Mihiel
notes: comprises 28 communes

properties.name: Saint-Mihiel
notes: Church of St. Etienne with sculpture by Ligier-Michier
communal college
public library
printing establishment

properties.name: San Pédor
notes: Has manufactories of cottons
Has distilleries of brandy

properties.name: Santo Lussurgin
notes: at the foot of the Menomeni
has a convent
has a distillery of brandy
carries on an active trade in cattle, cheese, and salted meats

properties.name: Sattarah
notes: fortress where Ram-Rajah was confined

properties.name: Schmadribach
notes: tributary of Lutschine

properties.name: Schwarze-Lutschine
notes: unites with Weisse-Lutschine at Zweilütschinen to form the Lutschine

properties.name: Staubbach
notes: tributary of Lutschine

properties.name: Thebes
notes: Site of ancient grandeur
Occupied the lower or N part of a broad fertile plain
Extended from the Libyan to the Arabian chain of mountains, a distance of perhaps 4 or 5 m.
Occupied both sides of the Nile

properties.name: Zweilütschinen
notes: confluence point of Weisse-Lutschine and Schwarze-Lutschine
